title: Data processing R codes for NET-seq results
author: Bao, Yu
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The R files documented three major steps in the work flow of E. coli-derived native elongating transcript sequencing (NET-seq) results. File 1 describes how to get a compiled text file of all NET-seq reads aligned to designated genome. File 2 describes a method to distinguish pause signals from background signals. File 3 describes how to use RNA secondary prediction to screen for putative hairpin-stabilized pause signals, a type of bacterial transcriptional pausing that is specifically strengthened by RNA stem-loop structures.
steps_raw: The R files documented three major steps in the work flow of E. coli-derived native elongating transcript sequencing (NET-seq) results. File 1 describes how to get a compiled text file of all NET-seq reads aligned to designated genome. File 2 describes a method to distinguish pause signals from background signals. File 3 describes how to use RNA secondary prediction to screen for putative hairpin-stabilized pause signals, a type of bacterial transcriptional pausing that is specifically strengthened by RNA stem-loop structures.
